items.summary: Project-side contract for declaring button elements (locators + component type + optional before/after hooks) that integrate with ROA’s ButtonService. Implement this as an enum (e.g., ButtonFields) so UI actions can target strongly-typed elements instead of raw Selenium locators.

usages.description: Define a project enum that implements ButtonUiElement to centralize Selenium locators and map each button to a concrete ButtonComponentType (e.g., Bootstrap). The before/after hooks are optional—use them only when a specific control needs extra stabilization (wait for presence/clickability) before interacting.
usages.contextHint: Project-side: keep button definitions in a single enum (e.g., ButtonFields) and map each constant to the correct ButtonComponentType so the framework selects the right component implementation. Add a before/after hook only for problematic/dynamic buttons; otherwise rely on the framework’s standard synchronization. Test-side usage is via fluent ButtonService methods (click/validate/etc.), which are documented under those method IDs.

items.summary: Single validation rule describing what to assert (target), how to assert it (type), and what value is expected. Assertions can be executed as soft (collected until quest.complete()) or hard (fail immediately) depending on the soft flag.

usages.code:
Assertion assertion = Assertion.builder()
    .target(UiTablesAssertionTarget.ROW_VALUES)
    .type(TableAssertionTypes.CONTAINS_ROW)
    .expected("TELECOM")
    .soft(true)
    .build();

quest.use(RING_OF_UI)
    .table().validate(Tables.ALL_TRANSACTIONS, assertion)
    .drop().complete();
usages.description: Build a table-focused assertion by combining a UI table AssertionTarget (what part of the table is validated) with a table AssertionType (how it’s validated) and an expected value. The table fluent validation executes the assertion and reports it through ROA’s assertion handling.
usages.contextHint: UI-side: Assertion is commonly used by table/validator flows (targets like UiTablesAssertionTarget and types like TableAssertionTypes). soft=true collects failures until quest.complete(); soft=false is typically treated as immediate failure by the validation layer.

items.summary: Base test class for sequential execution (PER_CLASS). Extend it when UI test methods must run one-by-one and/or share state across methods (e.g., reuse a logged-in session, keep a wizard flow alive, or avoid expensive re-setup per test).

usages.description: Extend BaseQuestSequential to execute tests sequentially within one class instance (PER_CLASS lifecycle).
usages.contextHint: Use when UI test methods must not run in parallel or when you intentionally share class state between tests. Prefer BaseQuest for normal parallel-friendly tests.

items.summary: Optional hook for one-time setup executed once before any @Test methods in a BaseQuestSequential class.

usages.description: Override to perform one-time setup for the whole test class.
usages.contextHint: Only applicable in classes extending BaseQuestSequential. Called automatically once before the first test method runs. Use Services to access Spring-managed beans if your setup needs them.

items.summary: Optional hook for one-time cleanup executed once after all @Test methods in a BaseQuestSequential class have finished.

usages.description: Override to perform one-time cleanup for the whole test class.
usages.contextHint: Only applicable in classes extending BaseQuestSequential. Called automatically once after the last test method finishes. Keep cleanup idempotent so reruns remain reliable.

items.summary: Base interface modeling a UI element: locator, enum impl, and pre/post hooks.

usages.description: Typical enum implementation to model stable page elements.
usages.contextHint: Keep selectors stable and centralized in enums implementing UiElement.

items.summary: Fluent service for input fields (type/insert, clear, read/getValue, state checks and validations). This service is part of the UI ring and is typically exposed through your project’s UiServiceFluent facade (e.g., AppUiService extends UiServiceFluent<AppUiService> and provides input() -> InputServiceFluent) so tests can call quest.use(RING_OF_UI).input() using enum-backed InputUiElement registries.

usages.description: Expose InputServiceFluent from your UiServiceFluent facade (AppUiService) so test code can enter the input fluent chain via quest.use(RING_OF_UI).input(). Then interact with inputs using enum-backed InputUiElement constants (InputFields.*) for insert/clear/value retrieval and validations, finishing with drop() and complete().
usages.contextHint: Framework-side: InputServiceFluent is wired by UiServiceFluent and should not be instantiated manually in tests. Project-side: expose it via AppUiService.input() (delegating to getInputField()). Test-side: prefer InputFields.* (InputUiElement) over raw By/WebElement.

items.summary: Validate that the current value of the input equals the expected value, with optional soft-assert behavior controlled by the boolean flag.

usages.code:
quest.use(RING_OF_UI)
  .input()
  .insert(InputFields.USERNAME_FIELD, "demo")
  .insert(InputFields.PASSWORD_FIELD, "demo")
  .validateValue(InputFields.USERNAME_FIELD, "demo", true)
  .validateValue(InputFields.PASSWORD_FIELD, "demo", true)
  .drop()
  .complete();
usages.description: Validate multiple input values in one flow. When soft=true the framework aggregates failures (useful for "check all fields" scenarios); when soft=false it fails fast like the hard overload.
usages.contextHint: Use soft=true to collect multiple field validation failures in one run. Avoid creating separate usages for true/false—this flag simply toggles aggregation vs fail-fast.

items.summary: Low-level insertion entrypoint that executes an input insertion using a raw locator + explicit ComponentType, passing optional insertion arguments. Intended for framework/component infrastructure rather than direct test usage.

usages.code:
quest.use(RING_OF_UI)
  .input()
  .insertion(InputFieldTypes.BOOTSTRAP_INPUT_TYPE, By.id("search"), new Object[] { "query" })
  .drop()
  .complete();
usages.description: Use when implementing or extending component infrastructure where you do not have (or intentionally cannot use) an enum-backed InputUiElement, but still need to execute the framework’s insertion strategy for a known component type and locator.
usages.contextHint: Prefer insert(InputUiElement, String) in normal @Test code. This method is mainly for framework extensions/adapters/components that operate on dynamic locators or metadata-driven controls.

items.summary: Validate that the input is enabled, with optional soft-assert behavior controlled by the boolean flag.

usages.code:
quest.use(RING_OF_UI)
  .input()
  .validateIsEnabled(InputFields.USERNAME_FIELD, true)
  .validateIsEnabled(InputFields.PASSWORD_FIELD, true)
  .drop()
  .complete();
usages.description: Validate enablement of multiple inputs in one chain. When soft=true failures are aggregated; when soft=false it behaves like a fail-fast assertion.
usages.contextHint: Use soft=true for “validate a whole form state” scenarios. If you only need a strict check, prefer validateIsEnabled(InputUiElement) for clarity.

items.summary: Evaluate whether the input is enabled and store the result into Quest storage for later use in the same test flow.

usages.code:
quest.use(RING_OF_UI)
  .input()
  .isEnabled(InputFields.PASSWORD_FIELD)
  .drop()
  .complete();
usages.description: Probe enablement when you need the value later (e.g., conditional branching or deferred assertions).
usages.contextHint: This stores the result in Quest storage (see the UI adapter’s storage extractors/keys). For straightforward tests, prefer validateIsEnabled(...) instead of storing and branching.

items.summary: Validate that the input is disabled, with optional soft-assert behavior controlled by the boolean flag.

usages.description: Validate disabled state of multiple inputs in one chain. When soft=true failures are aggregated; when soft=false it behaves like a fail-fast assertion.
usages.contextHint: Use soft=true to validate an entire form’s non-editable fields in one pass. Avoid creating separate usages for true/false—this flag simply toggles aggregation vs fail-fast.

items.summary: Hard-assert that the input is disabled (not editable).

usages.code:

  .drop()
  .complete();
usages.description: Fail immediately when a field must not be editable (e.g., computed or locked inputs).
usages.contextHint: Use when disabled state is a strict requirement before continuing. For grouped checks across many fields, use the soft-enabled overload with the boolean flag.

items.summary: Read the current validation/error message associated with an input and store it into Quest storage for later use in the same test flow.

usages.contextHint: This stores the error message in Quest storage (see the UI adapter’s storage extractors/keys). If you want to assert immediately, prefer validateErrorMessage(...) instead.

usages.contextHint: Use when the error message must match to proceed. If you want aggregated checks across many fields, use the soft-enabled overload with the boolean flag.

items.summary: Fluent service for select/dropdown controls (select option, read current selection/value, state checks and validations). This service is part of the UI ring and is typically exposed through your project’s UiServiceFluent facade (e.g., AppUiService extends UiServiceFluent<AppUiService> and provides select() -> SelectServiceFluent) so tests can call quest.use(RING_OF_UI).select() using enum-backed SelectUiElement registries.

usages.description: Expose SelectServiceFluent from your UiServiceFluent facade (AppUiService) so test code can enter the select fluent chain via quest.use(RING_OF_UI).select(). Then interact with dropdowns using enum-backed SelectUiElement constants (SelectFields.*) for selecting options and (when needed) validating the selected value, finishing with drop() and complete().
usages.contextHint: Framework-side: SelectServiceFluent is wired by UiServiceFluent and should not be instantiated manually in tests. Test-side: prefer SelectFields.* (SelectUiElement) over raw By/WebElement; validate selected options only when it matters to the scenario.
